title: Tips & Best Practices
summary: A collection of practical quick-wins for getting the best results from Hermes Agent, covering prompt writing, CLI power user features, context file usage, memory vs. skills, performance optimization, messaging tips, and security practices.

cli_commands.purpose: Resume last session with full conversation history

slash_commands.purpose: Summarize conversation history to reduce tokens
Usage patterns over the last 30 days
Cycle through tool output display modes: off → new → all → verbose
Set the home channel for cron job delivery

code_examples.caption: AGENTS.md example
code_examples.code:
# Project Context
- This is a FastAPI backend with SQLAlchemy ORM
- Always use async/await for database operations
- Tests go in tests/ and use pytest-asyncio
- Never commit .env files

code_examples.caption: SOUL.md example
code_examples.code:
# Soul
You are a senior backend engineer. Be terse and direct.
Skip explanations unless asked. Prefer one-liners over verbose solutions.
Always consider error handling and edge cases.

gotchas: Memory changes are written to disk immediately but won't appear in the system prompt until the next session starts
Don't break the prompt cache: keep context files and memory stable across turns to benefit from cache hits
Never set GATEWAY_ALLOW_ALL_USERS=true on a bot with terminal access
When running in a container backend, dangerous command checks are skipped — the container IS the security boundary
On Windows, open files with explicit encoding='utf-8' to avoid UnicodeEncodeError with non-Latin text
/verbose 'all' is great for watching agent work; 'off' is cleanest for simple Q&A
